briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for June 28, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Record-breaking Heatwaves Triggering a Global Emergency
sections.content: The European continent is experiencing continuous emergency declarations due to record-breaking heatwaves [1, 17], and is facing a severe crisis with Alpine glaciers melting and a surge in fatalities even before mid-summer [6, 8, 9]. Experts point out that such heatwaves in Europe would be impossible without climate change, and their frequency and intensity are increasing [47]. Southeast Asia is also suffering from 'humid heatwaves' that threaten human physiological limits [14, 18], and harsh climatic phenomena, such as record heatwaves, twin typhoons, and earthquakes occurring simultaneously, are being observed across the globe [39]. Furthermore, reports indicate that glacier lakes in British Columbia are at risk of collapse and trees are falling due to climate change [45, 48], demonstrating the global spread of the climate crisis's severity.

sections.title: ⚠️ Climate Change Threatening Domestic Ecosystems and Quality of Life
sections.content: Climate change is having a significant impact on domestic ecosystems. The value of domestic ecosystems, estimated at 34 trillion won annually, is threatened by habitat reduction due to climate change [3], and an analysis shows that over the past 30 years, urban areas have nearly doubled, leading to a decrease in agricultural land and wetlands, and increased pressure on ecosystems [40]. Furthermore, the increase in tropical nights leading to reduced sleep duration and increased sleep apnea highlights the 'butterfly effect' of climate change on public health [4]. This situation, coupled with international discussions emphasizing that investment in climate change adaptation is essential and the cost of inaction will be immense [44], calls for systematic responses across domestic ecosystems and society as a whole.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Domestic and International Adaptation Efforts to Address the Climate Crisis
sections.content: Amidst this climate crisis, various domestic and international efforts for climate change adaptation and carbon neutrality are expanding. Pocheon City is promoting the establishment of a climate change and carbon neutrality education center using closed schools [2], and the Korea Meteorological Administration is publishing climate change braille books to enhance understanding [7]. Innovative efforts in the agricultural sector to respond to climate change are also continuing, including the proposal of tax-exempt laws to support aquaculture restructuring and business closures [5, 11], and the development of smart farming and future income-generating crops in Goryeong-gun and Buyeo-gun [12, 13]. Alongside these, practical initiatives by local communities and businesses are active, such as the carbon neutrality resolution by eco-friendly farmers in Chungbuk [20, 28], strengthened citizen communication in Gwangsan-gu, Gwangju [27], and Lotte E&C's on-site safety inspections for heatwave preparedness [41]. The UN Secretary-General also urged an end to deforestation to curb global warming, emphasizing international solidarity [46].

sections.title: 🌱 Renewable Energy Transition, Need for Increased Speed and Efficiency
sections.content: Domestic and international interest and investment in the renewable energy transition, a core component of carbon neutrality, are also continuing. Samsung Electronics achieved a 95% renewable energy transition rate last year, leading eco-friendly management [34, 37], and Hyundai Steel is also planning to expand electric arc furnaces and increase renewable energy use [29]. The Asian Development Bank (ADB) is supporting the establishment of BESS (Battery Energy Storage Systems) in Cambodia, contributing to Southeast Asia's renewable energy transition [32], and at 'The Smarter E Europe' event, fossil fuels were declared a relic of the past, making the global message of transitioning to renewable energy clear [33]. However, domestically, the solar power deployment in the first half of the year amounted to only 2GW, signaling a red light for achieving the 2030 renewable energy target [31], and despite the growing role of railways in carbon neutrality, criticisms [25] about the unchanged electricity tariff system indicate that policy and institutional improvements remain urgent tasks.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the heatwaves sweeping across the globe, domestic and international efforts to address climate change, and the challenges of transitioning to renewable energy. Emphasizing once again the importance of comprehensive and cross-national responses in this era of climate crisis, this concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Urban Area Doubled in 30 Years, Farmland and Wetlands Decreased, Increasing Pressure on Ecosystems
articles.original_title: 도시 면적 30년 새 2배로···농경지·습지는 줄고 생태계 압력 커졌다
articles.description: Citizens with umbrellas are walking on a forest path in Incheon Grand Park on a day when spring rain fell nationwide. Senior Reporter Kim Jeong-geun. Over the past 30 years, as the domestic urban area nearly doubled, farmland and wetlands significantly decreased. It was analyzed that urbanization and the climate crisis have generally worsened the domestic ecosystem, with the risks of extreme heavy rainfall, wildfires, and landslides also increasing. The Ministry of Climate, Energy, and Environment's trends in Korea's ecosystem...

summary.phenomenon: Over the past 30 years, the domestic urban area has nearly doubled, and during the same period, farmland and wetlands have significantly decreased.
summary.cause: Urbanization and the climate crisis were analyzed to have generally worsened the domestic ecosystem.
summary.outlook: The risks of extreme heavy rainfall, wildfires, and landslides are expected to increase concurrently.
